summary: Frontend P1 verification for translation wiring fixes since iteration_35. All five test items PASS in NL. (1) Smoke check on /threats/16584b99 in NL — title 'Condensatievat - Slibopbouw' ✓, failure mode 'Slibopbouw' ✓, 'Bewerken' button ✓, 'Aangemaakt' date label ✓, sidebar tree still shows 'Lijn-90 / Lijn-60 / Nutvoorzieningen / Laboratorium / Hulpmiddel' ✓. (2) /equipment-manager page hierarchy tree now correctly renders translated Dutch node names: after expanding Tyromer → NLA → Lijn-90 / Lijn-60 / Nutvoorzieningen / Laboratorium / Hulpmiddel → Hefapparatuur → Vorkheftruck / GeMotoriseerde Palletheftruck → Acculader / GeMotoriseerde Palletheffer. Cross-checked drilling further into Lijn-90: 'EXU - Extrusie-eenheid' → '1R-2003 - Devulcanisatie Extruder' → 'Extruder Cilinder 1-12' / 'Extruder As Links'. All Dutch names from the equipment_node translation batch now render in the /equipment-manager tree (gap closed vs iteration_35). (3) PropertiesPanel header title uses translatedName — verified by clicking '1-Lijn-90' (header reads '1 - Lijn-90 / Section/System') and '1R-2003 - Devulcanisatie Extruder' (header reads '1R-2003 - Devulcanisat... / Subunit'). Description field couldn't be exercised on a node carrying a description because the specific Extruder id 961366ba (which the backend confirms has a Dutch description 'Geïmporteerd uit ongestructureerde lijst (bron: plakken)') was not surfaced under the visible tree — only its deeper Extruder Cilinder/Devulcanisatie Extruder descendants appeared. Code path is the same hook so wiring is presumed correct; flagged as a re-verify only if a node-with-description becomes reachable in UI. (4) Related Activity section on threat detail: section title now reads 'Gerelateerde activiteit (4)' ✓, filter button reads 'Alle (4)' ✓ (was 'All (4)'). Empty-state string 'Nog geen gerelateerde activiteit' could not be verified because this threat has 4 timeline cards (state not empty). (5) Recommended Actions section: title 'Aanbevolen acties' ✓ (was 'Recommended Actions'), 'Toevoegen' Add button ✓ (was 'Add'), hover 'Actie' button ✓ (was 'Act'). All three new observation keys (relatedActivity, act, addToActionPlan) plus common.added are correctly wired and rendering.

ui_bugs.issue: Tier badges still render English (Installation, Plant/Unit, Section/System, Equipment Unit, Subunit, Maintainable Item) on /equipment-manager — unchanged from iteration_35.
'Hierarchy', 'Tree', 'Levels', '234 items', 'Manage' segmented control + footer remain English under NL.
Each timeline card shows English 'Observation' label inside the colored chip even in NL (rest of the card is dynamic text). Static label likely needs t() wrapping.
Header subtitle still shows English tier (e.g. 'Subunit', 'Section/System') under translated name. Same root cause as the tier-badge issue.

ui_bugs.component: Risk Forecast tabs / Share dialog Close / LanguageSwitcher forwardRef
ui_bugs.issue: Pre-existing iteration_35 items still not addressed (7d/14d/30d tab labels English, Share dialog 'Close' button English in NL, React forwardRef console warning on LanguageSwitcher Button). Repeated for tracking only.

integration_issues.issue: Could not click into the canonical Extruder node id 961366ba (the one carrying a Dutch description) — UI surfaces only deeper Extruder Cilinder/Devulcanisatie Extruder descendants under EXU - Extrusie-eenheid; the sibling-level Extruder Section/System node was not exposed in the visible tree even after search. Code wiring exists at PropertiesPanel.js line 548 (translatedDescription) and uses the same hook proven to work for translatedName, so this is a UI reachability gap rather than a wiring gap.

action_items: (Optional) Surface the sibling-level Extruder Section/System node (id 961366ba) so the Dutch description 'Geïmporteerd uit ongestructureerde lijst (bron: plakken)' is reachable by user clicks — currently only its descendants appear after expanding Lijn-90 → EXU.

(Carry-over from iteration_35, still open) Translate tier badges (Installation/Plant/Unit/Section-System/Equipment Unit/Subunit/Maintainable Item), sidebar chrome (Hierarchy/Tree/Levels/Manage/N items), 'Observation' chip in timeline cards, 7d/14d/30d Risk Forecast tabs, Share dialog 'Close' button.
(Carry-over) Fix React forwardRef console warning on LanguageSwitcher trigger Button.
critical_code_review_comments: Translation wiring approach (useEquipmentNodeIdMap + translatedName/translatedDescription) is now consistently applied across both the sidebar EquipmentHierarchy.TreeNode and the /equipment-manager page tree — good consistency improvement.
Tier label strings (Installation, Plant/Unit, Section/System, Equipment Unit, Subunit, Maintainable Item) appear hard-coded as JSX rather than passed through t(); easy follow-up.

Several timeline/threat-detail static strings ('Observation' chip, Risk Forecast tab labels) still hard-coded as JSX; recommend a sweep to wrap remaining static labels in t() since the i18n harness now has full EN/NL/DE coverage.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on the 5 P1 items requested — iteration_35 smoke ✓, EquipmentManager tree translations ✓, PropertiesPanel translatedName ✓ (translatedDescription wiring presumed correct but not exercised), Related Activity title+filter ✓, Recommended Actions title+Add+Act ✓

context_for_next_testing_agent: All P1 translation wiring requested for iteration_36 verified PASS in NL. The /equipment-manager page tree now matches the sidebar EquipmentHierarchy (both translate node names). PropertiesPanel header title uses translatedName (verified on 'Lijn-90' and 'Devulcanisatie Extruder'). For end-to-end verification of translatedDescription, you need a node whose description column is non-empty — backend batch GET /api/translations/batch/equipment_node?language_code=nl confirms 961366ba carries the Dutch description, but the UI tree only exposes its descendants; consider testing via a direct route or by surfacing the Section/System-level Extruder node. Remaining gaps are tier badges + sidebar chrome strings; these are LOW priority static-string translations.
rca of the issue: n/a — no defects found in the in-scope P1 wiring.
